markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define safe batch rename, move, tag, folder color label, projection rebuild, and stale repair operations. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: DocumentBatchOperations leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: DocumentBatchOperations is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

evidence.finding: Move with empty body silently cleared project/folder membership; batch operations named by the contract were unimplemented (only per-doc rename/move)
evidence.fix: Move uses double-Option absent!=null semantics (absent=unchanged, null=clear, value=set); new bounded POST /knowledge/documents/batch (rename/move/set_authority_label) with per-item receipts + partial-failure reporting, write-gated by the MT-158 boundary. Remaining contract ops (tag, folder-color-label, projection-rebuild, stale-repair) are NOT implemented in this surface - tag edges exist via MT-155 backlinks, projection rebuild via MT-058 rebuild-status surfaces

findings.title: BATCH operations and most named ops are not implemented
findings.evidence: api routes expose only single-doc rename_document and move_document (api:786-853). The contract names batch rename, move, tag, folder color label, projection rebuild, and stale repair. There is no batch endpoint, no tag op, no folder-color op, no projection-rebuild op, no stale-repair op.
findings.fix: Implement batch endpoints (accept id lists, per-item receipts + partial-failure reporting) for the named operations, or descope the contract.

findings.title: move with an empty body silently clears project_ref and folder_ref
findings.evidence: move_document (api:820-837) passes body.project_ref/folder_ref (Option, default None) straight into move_knowledge_rich_document (knowledge.rs:4660 SET project_ref=$2, folder_ref=$3); a POST /move with empty body sets both to NULL, wiping membership.
findings.fix: Distinguish absent=leave unchanged from explicit null=clear (serde double-Option or separate clear flags).

claim_mismatches.claim: Define safe batch rename, move, tag, folder color label, projection rebuild, and stale repair operations
claim_mismatches.reality: only per-document rename and move exist; no batch, tag, folder-color, projection-rebuild, or stale-repair operation is implemented

missing_tests: test: batch rename of N docs with one failing id reports per-item results
test: move with empty body leaves existing project/folder unchanged

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-157 (DocumentBatchOperations) requires a concrete, restartable batch rename/move/authority-label surface with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and validator-readable evidence. The code delivers a real bounded POST /knowledge/documents/batch endpoint plus an absent-vs-null move fix, both proven against real PostgreSQL with per-item receipts, partial-failure reporting, and a server-derived write gate.
findings: Real batch endpoint: knowledge_documents.rs:1156-1278 (batch_documents) registered at routes line 106 (/knowledge/documents/batch). Bounded to BATCH_MAX_OPERATIONS=100, rejects empty list, applies rename/move/set_authority_label per-document with independent outcomes; one failing item never aborts the batch. Per-item save receipt recorded non-fatally (KnowledgeRichDocumentSaved). Response reports results[], succeeded, failed.

Move data-loss fix (prior adversarial-v2 MEDIUM): MoveBody now uses serde double-Option (knowledge_documents.rs:407-414 + double_option at 417-424). move_document (1118-1140) merges absent fields from current membership so an empty body is a no-op; explicit null clears one field; a string sets one field. Same law applied inside the batch Move arm (1196-1214). This fixes the prior silent-clear of both project_ref/folder_ref.

Real PostgreSQL proof: tests/knowledge_documents_api_tests.rs:770-903 (mt157_move_empty_body_preserves_membership_and_batch_reports_per_item) runs against knowledge_pg() which builds an isolated schema and runs all real migrations on a live cluster (knowledge_pg_support.rs:108-140); it SKIPs loudly only when PG binaries are absent. Asserts empty-body preserves PRJ-alpha/runbooks, null clears only folder, value sets only project, 3-op batch returns 200 with succeeded=1/failed=2 and per-item error kinds not_found/validation, post-batch durable title='Membership v2' and authority_label still 'promoted' (the bad 'published' label failed), and cloud_model batch returns 403.

Storage is genuine sqlx UPDATE against the PG pool: move_knowledge_rich_document (storage/knowledge.rs:5349-5371) and set_knowledge_rich_document_authority_label (5373+) enforce a closed authority_label vocabulary draft|promoted|archived, matching migration 0280 DEFAULT 'promoted' + CHECK constraint. No SQLite/in-memory authority.

Write gate is real and server-derived: ctx.require(DocumentAction::Write) at batch_documents start; actor_kind is validated strictly server-side (knowledge_documents.rs:240-262), missing header => least-privileged, unknown token => 400, cloud_model denied write => 403. No client-supplied privilege coercion.

No deferred-live escapes in the touched code (grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable returned nothing). The evidence honestly discloses that contract-named tag/folder-color-label/projection-rebuild/stale-repair ops are out of this surface and live in sibling MTs (MT-155 backlinks, MT-058 rebuild-status); this is an accurate scope statement, not a placeholder reachable from the implemented behavior.
Aside (non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; expected paperwork drift per review ground rules, not a defect.
evidence_checked: git show c8f54538 (impl + test diff) in wtc-kernel-009

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
